title: Protocol Outcomes, Layers, Standards, and Evidence · Runtime Contract: Layer Boundary Map
summary: A team says it built HTTP from scratch because one request works, but it cannot separate application semantics, wire syntax, transport behavior, implementation policy, or user and operator evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the layer boundary map increment in protocol outcome, layer, authority, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace application policy, HTTP semantics, HTTP/1.1 syntax, TLS records, TCP byte streams, IP delivery, and local socket APIs without collapsing layers.
Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Layer Boundary Map · Theory Layer Boundary Map
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 5418 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace application policy, HTTP semantics, HTTP/1.1 syntax, TLS records, TCP byte streams, IP delivery, and local socket APIs without collapsing layers.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Layer Boundary Map contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Layer Boundary Map · Theory Layer Boundary Map.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace application policy, http semantics, http/1.1 syntax, tls records, tcp byte streams, ip delivery, and local socket apis without collapsing layers. For Predict Layer Boundary Map · Theory Layer Boundary Map, keep Layer Boundary Map connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Repair the Layer Boundary Map defect · Theory Layer Boundary Map
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 5422 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace application policy, HTTP semantics, HTTP/1.1 syntax, TLS records, TCP byte streams, IP delivery, and local socket APIs without collapsing layers.
